title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Execution Trace: Feed Go Conflict Resolution
summary: Reading an entry mutates global source state, bulk operations partially apply silently, device updates overwrite each other, tags leak across tenants, and unread counts drift. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go conflict resolution increment in consistent personal reader-state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Use versions or timestamps deliberately for multi-device conflicts and distinguish stale writes from repeated commands.
Define bounded all-or-nothing or per-item bulk semantics, authorization, result reconciliation, and retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Conflict Resolution · Theory Feed Go Conflict Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5626 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use versions or timestamps deliberately for multi-device conflicts and distinguish stale writes from repeated commands.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Conflict Resolution contract for the supplied data.
